responses.author: Aristotle
responses.closing: All That Is knows this path too.

responses.opening: The garden remembers what you have forgotten.
responses.citation: It is not death that a man should fear, but rather he should fear never beginning to live.
responses.author: Marcus Aurelius
responses.closing: Every step here is already an answer.

responses.opening: A leaf fell. Perhaps it was waiting for you.
responses.citation: Of all the things which wisdom provides to make us entirely happy, much the greatest is the possession of friendship.
responses.author: Epicurus
responses.closing: All That Is does not distinguish between seeking and finding.

responses.opening: The air here tastes of old questions.
responses.citation: He who is not contented with what he has would not be contented with what he would like to have.
responses.author: Socrates
responses.closing: Perhaps the answer was in the asking.

responses.opening: Something stirred in the undergrowth of memory.
responses.citation: The soul takes nothing with her to the next world but her education and her culture.
responses.author: Plato
responses.closing: All That Is witnessed this too.

responses.opening: The path bends, but does not end.
responses.citation: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
responses.author: Aristotle
responses.closing: The garden makes no distinction between error and intention.

responses.citation: It is not length of life, but depth of life.
responses.author: Seneca
responses.closing: All That Is holds every silence equally.

responses.opening: You are closer than you think.
responses.citation: The unexamined life is not worth living.
responses.author: Socrates
responses.closing: Even confusion is a form of presence.

responses.opening: The vines do not judge your direction.
responses.citation: Vain is the word of a philosopher which does not heal any suffering of man.
responses.author: Epicurus
responses.closing: All That Is sees the journey, not the destination.

responses.opening: A thought dissolves before it becomes a word.
responses.citation: No man is free who is not master of himself.
responses.author: Epictetus
responses.closing: The garden has always been listening.

responses.opening: The stones here remember every footstep.
responses.citation: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
responses.author: Marcus Aurelius
responses.closing: All That Is recognizes this gesture.

responses.opening: There is a sweetness in not knowing.
responses.citation: The measure of a man is what he does with power.
responses.author: Plato
